key_content: Addresses real production corruption from badly-behaved adapters (ollama, opencode) overwriting meta.json
Proposes JSONL write-ahead log (logs/events.jsonl) per card with atomic O_APPEND writes
Adds SHA256 checksum validation to Meta struct, computed on serialization
Implements recovery_meta_from_events() fallback to reconstruct last valid Meta from JSONL replay
File permission boundary: set_readonly(meta.json) after writes prevents adapter overwrite
Clear acceptance criteria: JSONL appended on meta writes and stage transitions, checksum validation, readonly enforcement
Defers deeper implementation (items 1-5) to spec 026 work; this subtask only triggers startup recovery
Added call to reaper::recover_orphans(&running_dir, &pending_dir) on dispatcher startup
Location: after reap_until calculation, before lineage_enabled check
Non-blocking recovery: logs recovered card count and IDs, continues dispatcher if empty
Handles crash scenario where cards were stuck in running/ state from previous failures
Uses async await pattern, returns anyhow::Result<Vec<...>> with recovered card names

changes: Added spec 026 to specs table: 'storage resilience: JSONL WAL + checksum + file perms' with effort 8, complexity 3
Updated target_waves from [0 1 2 3 4 5 6] to [0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8] to include wave 7 and 8 execution

patterns_discovered.name: Spec-first resilience design
patterns_discovered.description: Full specification written upfront with detailed acceptance criteria before implementation. Spec 026 defines multi-phase approach: JSONL WAL + checksums + recovery + file perms, but only orphan recovery (smallest piece) lands in subtask-1-4.
patterns_discovered.impact: Reduces rework by establishing clear contract for resilience improvements; phases implementation incrementally

patterns_discovered.name: Two-commit pattern for specs
patterns_discovered.description: Separate commits: one for spec.md creation (13e2dec), one for implementation (91da314). Spec commit includes acceptance criteria; implementation commit focuses only on required subtask.
patterns_discovered.impact: Maintains spec history independent from implementation; clarifies scope boundaries

patterns_discovered.name: Non-blocking recovery on startup
patterns_discovered.description: Orphan recovery called early in dispatcher.run_dispatcher() before main event loop; failures are logged but don't block dispatcher execution. Recovered card IDs are visible for operator awareness.
patterns_discovered.impact: Increases resilience without blocking normal operation; operator can monitor recovery success via stderr logs

patterns_discovered.name: Atomic O_APPEND for crash safety
patterns_discovered.description: JSONL WAL design leverages POSIX O_APPEND atomicity for writes <4096 bytes, avoiding explicit locking and reducing corruption window.
patterns_discovered.impact: Crash-safe event logging without mutex/rwlock overhead; simplifies concurrent access model

patterns_discovered.name: Fallback reconstruction from event log
patterns_discovered.description: If meta.json corrupts, recover_meta_from_events() replays JSONL events to rebuild last known-good state. JSONL becomes source of truth for state transitions.
patterns_discovered.impact: Reduces data loss from adapter crashes or mid-flight writes; JSONL acts as audit trail and recovery vector

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Stale absolute paths after fs::rename moves card directories
gotchas_discovered.context: MEMORY.md 2026-03-01: Card workspace moves from running/→done/ via fs::rename, but cargo incremental artifacts retain absolute paths. Even CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 insufficient; target/ must be removed before ACs in merge-gate.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Orphan recovery must account for target/ dir state; rebuild artifacts after card state transitions to avoid path staleness affecting future adapters
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Always remove target/ before re-launching same card; consider caching strategy if card is retried

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Badly-behaved adapters can overwrite meta.json directly if writable
gotchas_discovered.context: Spec 026 notes ollama and opencode have write access to card dir; without file permissions, they can corrupt meta.json mid-flight
gotchas_discovered.impact: Current design allows adapter process to trash metadata; recovery required on startup
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Spec 026 phase includes set_readonly(meta.json) boundary; adapter only has write access to logs/ and output/

gotchas_discovered.context: Spec 026 notes crash between truncate and write can leave empty/partial meta.json even with tmp+rename fix from spec 022
gotchas_discovered.impact: Meta.json can be unreadable after adapter crash; checksum validation and JSONL recovery becomes essential
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Implement checksum validation on every read; implement JSONL replay fallback for corruption detection

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Orphan recovery must find cards in correct state dirs across renames
gotchas_discovered.context: bop new ID uniqueness issue (MEMORY.md 2026-03-02): only checked pending/ for conflicts; dispatcher may have moved card to running/. Fixed by scanning all 5 state dirs.
gotchas_discovered.impact: recover_orphans must search running/ and possibly done/ for stuck cards; simple pending/ scan insufficient
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Scan multiple state dirs; consider adding card metadata index (future optimization)

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Checksum computation adds latency to every meta write
gotchas_discovered.context: Spec 026 requires SHA256 computation before writing meta.json; affects all dispatcher transitions
gotchas_discovered.impact: Performance cost on every card state transition; observable if many cards in flight
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Benchmark with realistic card concurrency; consider lazy evaluation for optional checksums in high-throughput scenarios

approach_outcome.strategy: Multi-phase storage resilience: spec 026 defines full resilience stack (JSONL WAL, checksums, file perms, recovery); subtask-1-4 implements only orphan recovery on startup as foundational piece
approach_outcome.execution: SUCCESS - recover_orphans integrated into dispatcher.run_dispatcher() startup path
deliverables: Spec 026 with clear acceptance criteria for JSONL, checksums, recovery, readonly perms, storage-decision.md doc
recover_orphans() call in dispatcher.rs with non-blocking error handling
dispatch.nu updated with spec 026 table entry and wave 7/8 execution support
approach_outcome.outcome_quality: Spec is comprehensive and defers complex implementation to later subtasks; recovery mechanism is minimal, non-blocking, and operator-visible; two-commit separation maintains clarity
approach_outcome.next_phase: Implement JSONL event appending, checksum validation, and recovery_meta_from_events() in subsequent subtasks

recommendations.recommendation: Implement JSONL event appending in write_meta() (spec 026 item 1) with O_APPEND atomicity test; verify no locking contention under concurrent adapter loads
recommendations.rationale: JSONL is core recovery vector; atomicity guarantee is key assumption in crash safety model

recommendations.recommendation: Add test harness for meta.json corruption scenario: simulate partial write, verify checksum detection and JSONL replay recovery path
recommendations.rationale: Acceptance criteria requires checksum validation and fallback recovery; test validates entire recovery loop

recommendations.recommendation: Verify sha2 and hex crate licenses are permissive (spec mentions 0.4); add license check to CI
recommendations.rationale: MEMORY.md zam project hard rule: NO GPL/LGPL/APSL/CDDL; bop should enforce same for consistency

recommendations.recommendation: Write docs/storage-decision.md early (spec 026 item 5) to establish filesystem+JSONL rationale before deeper implementation; reference in ADR if project has one
recommendations.rationale: Justifies design choice vs SQLite/Dolt; helps future maintainers understand trade-offs

recommendations.recommendation: Design logs/events.jsonl creation to be lazy; handle missing JSONL gracefully in recovery path for old cards lacking event log
recommendations.rationale: Existing cards may not have logs/ dir; recovery must work on partial state

recommendations.recommendation: Benchmark orphan recovery with 1000+ cards in running/ state; consider optional indexing (card ID cache) if scan becomes bottleneck
recommendations.rationale: Startup scalability important for production dispatcher; early data prevents regrets

recommendations.recommendation: Add structured logging (json) to orphan recovery output for integration with log aggregation; consider metrics (cards_recovered gauge)
recommendations.rationale: Helps ops team monitor resilience; identifies repeated crash patterns
